response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad web and full-stack engineering background with meaningful geospatial side projects, but the role demands production-grade modern C++ or Rust expertise applied to high-throughput routing data pipelines — an area where this resume falls well short. His C++ usage is limited to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, with no Rust at all. His GIS work (GISCollective, Green Map) is hobbyist/NGO-scale and web-centric rather than routing tile generation or navigation pipeline engineering. The distributed systems experience at Sociomantic is the closest match but is years old and TypeScript/DLang-based, not C++. There is no evidence of routing algorithms, tile formats, real-time traffic integration, or navigation guidance systems.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective, Green Map GIS app, and a FOSS4G conference talk — establishes baseline domain familiarity
Distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates understanding of high-throughput, low-latency service design
Broad polyglot background (DLang, C++, Node.js, TypeScript, Java, Swift) shows adaptability and willingness to cross language boundaries
concerns: No meaningful modern C++ and zero Rust experience — the two primary required technologies; C++ usage is a decade old and non-pipeline in nature
Geospatial work is entirely web/GIS-collection oriented with no evidence of routing tiles, map data transformation, navigation guidance, or pipeline latency optimization
Career trajectory is firmly in web and full-stack product engineering (TypeScript, React, Node.js, MongoDB) with no recent pivot toward systems-level or geospatial infrastructure work
